mnemonics: MYTHS: Managers Yearn To Hasten Schedules. (Highlights the common management myth that adding people late speeds up a project).
keyPoints: Myths are often misleading and over-simplified beliefs that can hinder good software engineering practices.
They exist in three main categories: Management Myths, Customer Myths, and Practitioner's Myths.
Believing in these myths can lead to poor planning, unrealistic expectations, and low-quality software.
commonMistakes: Believing that adding more programmers to a late project will speed it up (Brooks' Law).
Assuming that a general statement of requirements is enough to begin writing code, leading to extensive rework.
